name: grill-me
description: Relentless one-question-at-a-time Socratic interview that stress-tests a plan, design, or decision BEFORE any code is written. Every question now offers an ordered top 3-4 answer options + a free-type slot. Maps the decision tree, resolves the highest-impact unknowns first, explores the codebase instead of asking when answers are discoverable, surfaces dependencies, pushes back on risky or contradictory choices, and ends in a structured shared understanding. Basic mode generates the options inline; opt-in HARD mode ("grill me hard") assembles the whole question list, runs one tournament workflow (grill-hard.workflow.js) to pre-compute ranked options per question, then runs the same drift-aware interview. Planning-only. Use when someone says "grill me" / "grill me hard", or wants to poke holes in / pressure-test / red-team / pre-mortem a plan before building.
